metadata.title: English Dictionary
metadata.description: A comprehensive offline English dictionary with meanings, parts of speech, and examples.

words.meaning: To leave completely and finally; to give up utterly.
words.example: She had to abandon her car in the flood.

words.meaning: The power or skill to do something.
words.example: He has the ability to speak five languages.

words.meaning: To formally put an end to a system, practice, or institution.
words.example: The government voted to abolish the death penalty.

words.meaning: To take in or soak up energy, liquid, or another substance.
words.example: The sponge absorbs water quickly.

words.meaning: Existing in thought or as an idea but not having a physical existence.
words.example: Mathematics deals with abstract concepts.

words.meaning: Existing or available in large quantities; more than enough.
words.example: The region has abundant natural resources.

words.meaning: To consent to receive or undertake something offered.
words.example: She accepted the job offer.

words.meaning: Correct in all details; exact.
words.example: The report was accurate and well-researched.

words.meaning: To successfully bring about or reach a desired objective.
words.example: He worked hard to achieve his goals.

words.meaning: To accept or admit the existence or truth of something.
words.example: She acknowledged her mistake openly.

words.meaning: To buy or obtain an asset or object for oneself.
words.example: They acquired new skills during the course.

words.meaning: To make suitable for a new use or purpose; to adjust.
words.example: Animals adapt to their environment over time.

words.meaning: Satisfactory or acceptable in quality or quantity.
words.example: The food supply was adequate for all guests.

words.meaning: To regard with respect or warm approval.
words.example: Everyone admired her courage and dedication.

words.meaning: To legally take another's child and bring it up as one's own.
words.example: They decided to adopt a child.

words.meaning: To move forward in a purposeful way.
words.example: Science continues to advance at a rapid pace.

words.meaning: Difficult or unpleasant circumstances; misfortune.
words.example: She showed great strength in the face of adversity.

words.meaning: To publicly recommend or support a particular cause.
words.example: She advocates for equal rights.

words.meaning: Concerned with beauty or the appreciation of beauty.
words.example: The room had a pleasing aesthetic quality.

words.meaning: A gentle feeling of fondness or liking.
words.example: She showed great affection for her pets.

words.meaning: Having a great deal of money; wealthy.
words.example: They lived in an affluent neighborhood.

words.meaning: To make a problem or situation worse or more serious.
words.example: Stress can aggravate health problems.

words.meaning: Able to move quickly and easily; nimble.
words.example: The gymnast was incredibly agile.

words.meaning: Watchful and prepared for any danger or difficulty.
words.example: Security guards must remain alert at all times.

words.meaning: To make suffering or a problem less severe.
words.example: Medicine can help alleviate pain.

words.meaning: The quality of being powerfully and mysteriously attractive.
words.example: The allure of the city drew many visitors.

words.meaning: Showing a selfless concern for the well-being of others.
words.example: Her altruistic nature led her to volunteer.

words.meaning: Open to more than one interpretation; not clear.
words.example: The instructions were ambiguous and confusing.

words.meaning: A strong desire to do or achieve something.
words.example: His ambition was to become a doctor.

words.meaning: To examine something in detail in order to understand it.
words.example: Scientists analyze data to find patterns.

words.meaning: A heavy object used to keep a ship in one place.
words.example: The ship dropped its anchor near the shore.

words.meaning: Belonging to the very distant past.
words.example: They explored ancient ruins in Greece.

words.meaning: To regard as probable; to expect or predict.
words.example: We anticipate a large crowd at the event.

words.meaning: A feeling of worry, nervousness, or unease.
words.example: She felt anxiety before the big exam.

words.meaning: Clearly visible or understood; obvious.
words.example: It was apparent that he was telling the truth.

words.meaning: To make a serious or urgent request.
words.example: The charity appealed for donations.

words.meaning: To recognize the full worth of something or someone.
words.example: I really appreciate your help.

words.meaning: Suitable or proper in the circumstances.
words.example: Wear appropriate clothing for the occasion.

words.meaning: To have a strong desire or ambition to achieve something.
words.example: She aspires to be a renowned scientist.

words.meaning: To state a fact or belief confidently.
words.example: He asserted his innocence firmly.

words.meaning: To evaluate or estimate the nature or quality of something.
words.example: The teacher assessed the students' progress.

words.meaning: A useful or valuable thing or person.
words.example: Her creativity is a great asset to the team.

words.meaning: To suppose to be the case without proof.
words.example: Don't assume things without evidence.

words.meaning: To tell someone something positively to dispel doubt.
words.example: I assure you, everything will be fine.

words.meaning: The envelope of gases surrounding the earth.
words.example: Pollution damages the atmosphere.

words.meaning: To succeed in achieving something.
words.example: She attained the highest score in class.

words.meaning: A quality or feature regarded as a characteristic.
words.example: Patience is an important attribute for teachers.

words.meaning: Showing a willingness to take surprisingly bold risks.
words.example: His audacious plan surprised everyone.

words.meaning: The power or right to give orders and make decisions.
words.example: The manager has authority over his team.

words.meaning: Having knowledge or perception of a situation or fact.
words.example: Be aware of your surroundings.

words.meaning: An even distribution of weight enabling someone to remain upright.
words.example: She maintained her balance on the tightrope.

words.meaning: A fence or obstacle that prevents movement or access.
words.example: Language can be a barrier to communication.

words.meaning: An advantage or profit gained from something.
words.example: Regular exercise has many health benefits.

words.meaning: Well-meaning and kindly disposed.
words.example: The benevolent king cared for his people.

words.meaning: Prejudice in favor of or against one thing over another.
words.example: Judges must not show any bias.

words.meaning: Large in quantity; abundant.
words.example: They had a bountiful harvest this year.

words.meaning: Concise and exact use of words in writing or speech.
words.example: Brevity is the soul of wit.

words.meaning: Exceptionally clever or talented.
words.example: She is a brilliant mathematician.

words.meaning: To determine mathematically; to compute.
words.example: He calculated the total cost of the project.

words.meaning: Truthful and straightforward; frank.
words.example: She gave a candid answer to the question.

words.meaning: The power or ability to do something.
words.example: This software has great capability for design.

words.meaning: To attract and hold the interest and attention of.
words.example: The performer captivated the audience.

words.meaning: A person or thing that gives rise to an action or condition.
words.example: The cause of the accident was reckless driving.

words.meaning: A task or situation that tests someone's abilities.
words.example: Learning a new language is a big challenge.

words.meaning: The quality of being clear and easy to understand.
words.example: She spoke with great clarity and confidence.

words.meaning: To work jointly with others on a project.
words.example: The two companies collaborated on the new product.

words.meaning: Sympathetic concern for the sufferings of others.
words.example: The nurse showed great compassion for her patients.

words.meaning: Evoking interest, attention, or admiration powerfully.
words.example: She made a compelling argument.

words.meaning: Consisting of many different and connected parts.
words.example: The issue is far more complex than it appears.

words.meaning: An abstract idea or general notion.
words.example: The concept of democracy is widely discussed.

words.meaning: Giving a lot of information clearly in few words.
words.example: Write a concise summary of the chapter.

words.meaning: The feeling or belief that one can rely on someone or something.
words.example: She answered all questions with confidence.

words.meaning: A serious disagreement or argument.
words.example: The conflict between the two nations lasted years.

words.meaning: To overcome and take control of something by force.
words.example: She conquered her fear of heights.

words.meaning: Aware of and responding to one's surroundings.
words.example: He became conscious after the surgery.

words.meaning: A result or effect of an action or condition.
words.example: Think about the consequences before deciding.

words.meaning: Acting or done in the same way over time.
words.example: Consistent practice leads to improvement.

words.meaning: To build or erect something.
words.example: They constructed a new bridge over the river.

words.meaning: To give something to help achieve or provide something.
words.example: Everyone can contribute to society in their own way.

words.meaning: To cause someone to believe something is true.
words.example: She convinced him to try the new restaurant.

words.meaning: The ability to do something frightening; bravery.
words.example: It takes courage to stand up for what is right.

words.meaning: Relating to or involving the use of imagination.
words.example: The artist had a very creative mind.

words.meaning: Able to be believed; convincing.
words.example: She is a credible witness.

words.meaning: Decisive or critical, especially in the success of something.
words.example: It is crucial that you follow the instructions.

words.meaning: Eager to know or learn something.
words.example: Children are naturally curious about the world.

words.meaning: To devote time or effort to a particular task.
words.example: She dedicated her life to helping others.

words.meaning: Done consciously and intentionally.
words.example: His deliberate actions showed careful planning.

words.meaning: To clearly show the existence or truth of something.
words.example: He demonstrated how to use the equipment.

words.meaning: To be determined or controlled by something else.
words.example: Success depends on hard work and dedication.

words.meaning: To represent in a picture or other artwork.
words.example: The painting depicts a busy market scene.

words.meaning: To obtain something from a specified source.
words.example: He derives great pleasure from reading.

words.meaning: To have earned something by one's actions or qualities.
words.example: She deserves the award for her hard work.

words.meaning: To ascertain or establish exactly by research.
words.example: Scientists are working to determine the cause of the disease.

words.meaning: To grow or cause to grow and become more advanced.
words.example: The company plans to develop new software.

words.meaning: Having or showing care in one's work or duties.
words.example: She is a diligent student who studies every day.

words.meaning: To find unexpectedly or in the course of a search.
words.example: Columbus discovered America in 1492.

words.meaning: Recognizably different in nature from something else.
words.example: The two cultures have distinct traditions.

words.meaning: Showing a great deal of variety; very different.
words.example: The city has a diverse population.

words.meaning: To have a commanding influence on; to control.
words.example: The team dominated the game from start to finish.

words.meaning: Characterized by constant change, activity, or progress.
words.example: The tech industry is very dynamic.

words.meaning: Strongly wanting to do or have something.
words.example: She was eager to start her new job.

words.meaning: Successful in producing a desired or intended result.
words.example: The medicine was highly effective.

words.meaning: Achieving maximum productivity with minimum wasted effort.
words.example: An efficient worker completes tasks quickly.

words.meaning: Involving many carefully arranged parts; detailed.
words.example: She wore an elaborate costume to the party.

words.meaning: To raise to a higher position or level.
words.example: Hard work can elevate your career.

words.meaning: To completely remove or get rid of something.
words.example: We need to eliminate waste in our processes.

words.meaning: Fluent or persuasive in speaking or writing.
words.example: The senator gave an eloquent speech.

words.meaning: To come into being or prominence.
words.example: A new leader emerged from the group.

words.meaning: The ability to understand and share the feelings of another.
words.example: Empathy is an important trait for caregivers.

words.meaning: To give authority or power to someone to do something.
words.example: Education empowers individuals to succeed.

words.meaning: To give support, confidence, or hope to someone.
words.example: Teachers should encourage students to ask questions.

words.meaning: To suffer something difficult patiently.
words.example: She endured years of hardship without complaint.

words.meaning: To intensify, increase, or further improve the quality of something.
words.example: Regular exercise enhances your physical health.

words.meaning: Very large in size, quantity, or extent.
words.example: The elephant was enormous compared to the other animals.

words.meaning: Intense and eager enjoyment or interest.
words.example: She approached the project with great enthusiasm.

words.meaning: Absolutely necessary; extremely important.
words.example: Water is essential for life.

words.meaning: To form an idea of the amount or value of; to assess.
words.example: We need to evaluate the risks before proceeding.

words.meaning: Plain or obvious; clearly seen or understood.
words.example: It was evident that she was upset.

words.meaning: To develop gradually over time.
words.example: Technology continues to evolve rapidly.

words.meaning: To be exceptionally good at or proficient in something.
words.example: She excels at mathematics and science.

words.meaning: To become or make larger or more extensive.
words.example: The company plans to expand into new markets.

words.meaning: Stated clearly and in detail, leaving no room for confusion.
words.example: The instructions were explicit and easy to follow.

words.meaning: To travel in or through an unfamiliar area in order to learn about it.
words.example: They decided to explore the jungle.

words.meaning: To convey a thought or feeling in words or actions.
words.example: She expressed her gratitude with a kind note.

words.meaning: Very unusual or remarkable.
words.example: She has an extraordinary talent for music.

words.meaning: To make an action or process easier.
words.example: Technology facilitates communication globally.

words.meaning: To grow or develop in a healthy way.
words.example: Plants flourish in the spring sun.

words.meaning: To pay particular attention to something.
words.example: Focus on your goals and work toward them.

words.meaning: Inspiring fear or respect through being impressively capable.
words.example: She is a formidable opponent in chess.

words.meaning: An underlying basis or principle for something.
words.example: Trust is the foundation of every good relationship.

words.meaning: The power or right to act, speak, or think freely.
words.example: Freedom of speech is a fundamental right.

words.meaning: Forming a necessary base; of central importance.
words.example: Reading is a fundamental skill for all students.

words.meaning: To produce or create something.
words.example: Solar panels generate electricity from sunlight.

words.meaning: Showing readiness to give more than is expected.
words.example: She was generous with her time and resources.

words.meaning: Truly what something is said to be; authentic.
words.example: His concern for others was genuine.

words.meaning: Relating to the whole world; worldwide.
words.example: Climate change is a global issue.

words.meaning: The quality of being thankful; readiness to show appreciation.
words.example: She expressed gratitude for his support.

words.meaning: To show or indicate the way; to direct.
words.example: The teacher guided students through the lesson.

words.meaning: The combination of simultaneously sounded musical notes to produce a pleasing effect.
words.example: The choir sang in perfect harmony.

words.meaning: Property that is or may be inherited; valued things passed down.
words.example: India has a rich cultural heritage.

words.meaning: Having a modest or low estimate of one's own importance.
words.example: Despite his success, he remained humble.

words.meaning: To establish or indicate who or what someone or something is.
words.example: Can you identify the source of the problem?

words.meaning: To light up; to make something clearer or easier to understand.
words.example: The teacher's explanation illuminated the concept.

words.meaning: Having or showing creativity or inventiveness.
words.example: She is an imaginative and gifted writer.

words.meaning: A marked effect or influence.
words.example: The charity had a huge impact on the community.

words.meaning: To put a plan or decision into effect.
words.example: The company implemented a new policy.

words.meaning: Of great significance or value.
words.example: It is important to stay hydrated.

words.meaning: To make or become better.
words.example: Practice daily to improve your skills.

words.meaning: A thing that motivates or encourages someone to do something.
words.example: The bonus was an incentive for better performance.

words.meaning: To comprise or contain as part of a whole.
words.example: The package includes accommodation and meals.

words.meaning: The fact of being free from outside control.
words.example: Children need to develop independence as they grow.

words.meaning: The capacity to have an effect on the character of someone.
words.example: Parents have a strong influence on their children.

words.meaning: Featuring new methods; advanced and original.
words.example: The company launched an innovative new product.

words.meaning: To fill someone with the urge to do or feel something.
words.example: Her story inspired many people to follow their dreams.

words.meaning: The quality of being honest and having strong moral principles.
words.example: She is known for her integrity and fairness.

words.meaning: The ability to acquire and apply knowledge and skills.
words.example: Intelligence is not just about academic performance.

words.meaning: To carry out a systematic inquiry to find out facts.
words.example: Police are investigating the incident.

words.meaning: Just behaviour or treatment; fairness.
words.example: Everyone deserves justice under the law.

words.meaning: Facts, information, and skills acquired through experience.
words.example: Knowledge is power.

words.meaning: The action of leading a group of people.
words.example: Strong leadership is vital in a crisis.

words.meaning: Of or according to the rules of logic; reasonable.
words.example: She made a logical argument for her position.

words.meaning: A strong feeling of support or allegiance.
words.example: His loyalty to his friends was admirable.

words.meaning: Impressively beautiful, elaborate, or extravagant.
words.example: The view from the mountain was magnificent.

words.meaning: To cause or enable a condition or situation to continue.
words.example: You need to maintain a healthy lifestyle.

words.meaning: Having a serious, important, or useful quality.
words.example: She had a meaningful conversation with her mentor.

words.meaning: To provide with a reason to act in a particular way.
words.example: A good teacher knows how to motivate students.

words.meaning: To plan and direct the course of a journey.
words.example: She used the map to navigate through the city.

words.meaning: Having or showing fine personal qualities or high moral principles.
words.example: It was a noble act of sacrifice.

words.meaning: To care for and encourage the growth or development of.
words.example: Good parents nurture their children's talents.

words.meaning: To notice or perceive something and register it as being significant.
words.example: Scientists observe natural phenomena carefully.

words.meaning: A set of circumstances that makes it possible to do something.
words.example: She seized the opportunity to study abroad.

words.meaning: Hopeful and confident about the future.
words.example: He remained optimistic despite the challenges.

words.meaning: To arrange into a structured whole; to put in order.
words.example: She organized all the files on her computer.

words.meaning: Present or existing from the beginning; first or earliest.
words.example: The original version of the song is much better.

words.meaning: To succeed in dealing with a problem or difficulty.
words.example: He overcame many obstacles to succeed.

words.meaning: Having or showing strong feelings, enthusiasm, or belief.
words.example: She is passionate about environmental conservation.

words.meaning: The capacity to accept delay or trouble without getting angry.
words.example: Patience is essential when learning a new skill.

words.meaning: To become aware of something through the senses.
words.example: He perceived a change in her attitude.

words.meaning: Continuing firmly despite difficulty or opposition.
words.example: Her persistent efforts paid off in the end.

words.meaning: A particular attitude toward something; a point of view.
words.example: Travel can give you a new perspective on life.

words.meaning: A person who is among the first to explore or settle a new area.
words.example: Marie Curie was a pioneer in science.

words.meaning: Latent qualities or abilities that may be developed.
words.example: Every child has the potential to succeed.

words.meaning: Marked by exactness and accuracy of expression or detail.
words.example: The scientist gave a precise measurement.

words.meaning: To maintain something in its original state.
words.example: We must preserve our natural environment.

words.meaning: The fact of being regarded as more important than another.
words.example: Health should always be a priority.

words.meaning: Forward movement toward a destination or goal.
words.example: The team made significant progress on the project.

words.meaning: To support or actively encourage something; to further.
words.example: The campaign promotes healthy eating habits.

words.meaning: To succeed in material terms; to flourish financially.
words.example: The business began to prosper after the launch.

words.meaning: To follow or chase; to continue or proceed along a path.
words.example: She decided to pursue a career in medicine.

words.meaning: The standard of something as measured against other things.
words.example: The quality of the product was excellent.

words.meaning: Sending out light; shining or glowing brightly.
words.example: She had a radiant smile that lit up the room.

words.meaning: Having or showing a sensible and practical idea of what can be achieved.
words.example: Be realistic about what you can accomplish.

words.meaning: To identify from having encountered before.
words.example: I didn't recognize him at first.

words.meaning: To think deeply or carefully about something.
words.example: Take time to reflect on your experiences.

words.meaning: Worthy of attention; striking or extraordinary.
words.example: She achieved a remarkable feat at such a young age.

words.meaning: The capacity to recover quickly from difficulties.
words.example: Her resilience helped her bounce back after failure.

words.meaning: A feeling of deep admiration for someone or something.
words.example: Show respect to your elders.

words.meaning: The state or fact of having a duty to deal with something.
words.example: It is your responsibility to complete the task.

words.meaning: To return something to a former condition.
words.example: Workers restored the old painting carefully.

words.meaning: To make previously unknown or secret information known.
words.example: The investigation revealed the truth.

words.meaning: Extremely thorough and careful.
words.example: The training program was rigorous and demanding.

words.meaning: An act of giving up something valued for the sake of something else.
words.example: Parents make sacrifices for their children.

words.meaning: Sufficiently great or important to be worthy of attention.
words.example: She made a significant contribution to the project.

words.meaning: Free from pretense or deceit; genuine.
words.example: He offered a sincere apology.

words.meaning: A means of solving a problem or dealing with a difficult situation.
words.example: We need to find a solution to this issue.

words.meaning: Having a refined knowledge of the ways of the world.
words.example: The design was sophisticated and elegant.

words.meaning: Relating to the identification of long-term goals.
words.example: The company made a strategic decision to expand.

words.meaning: The quality or state of being physically strong.
words.example: Regular exercise builds strength and stamina.

words.meaning: To make great efforts to achieve or obtain something.
words.example: Always strive to be the best version of yourself.

words.meaning: Of considerable importance, size, or worth.
words.example: She made a substantial improvement in her grades.

words.meaning: To achieve the desired aim or result.
words.example: Hard work is key to succeed in life.

words.meaning: To bear all or part of the weight of; to hold up.
words.example: Friends support each other in difficult times.

words.meaning: To keep in existence; to maintain.
words.example: We must sustain our efforts to reduce pollution.

words.meaning: Natural aptitude or skill.
words.example: She has a natural talent for painting.

words.meaning: Complete with regard to every detail; not superficial.
words.example: He did a thorough job of cleaning the house.

words.meaning: Absorbed in or involving thought; showing consideration.
words.example: She gave a thoughtful gift to her friend.

words.meaning: To make a marked change in the form or nature of something.
words.example: Technology has transformed the way we communicate.

words.meaning: Easy to perceive or detect; not deceptive.
words.example: The company is transparent about its practices.

words.meaning: A great victory or achievement.
words.example: Winning the championship was a great triumph.

words.meaning: Being the only one of its kind; unlike anything else.
words.example: Every person has a unique set of skills.

words.meaning: Worth a great deal of money or importance.
words.example: Her feedback was very valuable to the team.

words.meaning: Able to adapt or be adapted to many different functions.
words.example: She is a versatile actress who can play any role.

words.meaning: Full of energy and enthusiasm.
words.example: The city has a vibrant nightlife and culture.

words.meaning: Keeping careful watch for possible danger or difficulties.
words.example: Be vigilant when walking alone at night.

words.meaning: The ability to think about the future with imagination.
words.example: The leader had a clear vision for the organization.

words.meaning: The quality of having experience and good judgment.
words.example: With age comes wisdom.

words.meaning: Great energy or enthusiasm in pursuit of a cause.
words.example: She approached every task with great zeal.

words.meaning: Present or experienced to a severe or intense degree.
words.example: He had acute pain in his chest.

words.meaning: Very skilled or proficient at something.
words.example: She is adept at solving complex problems.

words.meaning: To state as a fact; to assert strongly.
words.example: He affirmed his commitment to the project.

words.meaning: Extreme physical or mental suffering.
words.example: She was in agony after twisting her ankle.

words.meaning: To distribute resources or duties for a specific purpose.
words.example: The budget was allocated to different departments.

words.meaning: To make changes to improve something.
words.example: The law was amended to include new provisions.

words.meaning: More than enough; plentiful.
words.example: There was ample time to complete the task.

words.meaning: A short interesting story about a real event.
words.example: The teacher shared a funny anecdote about her travels.

words.meaning: Appropriate or suitable in the circumstances.
words.example: It was an apt description of the situation.

words.meaning: Involving or requiring strenuous effort; difficult.
words.example: The mountain trek was arduous but rewarding.

words.meaning: Having or showing the ability to speak fluently and coherently.
words.example: She is a very articulate speaker.

words.meaning: To go up; to climb or rise.
words.example: They slowly ascended the steep mountain path.

words.meaning: A hope or ambition of achieving something.
words.example: Her aspiration was to become a world-renowned artist.

words.meaning: Having an ability to accurately assess situations; shrewd.
words.example: She made an astute business decision.

words.meaning: Of undisputed origin; genuine.
words.example: The restaurant serves authentic Italian cuisine.

words.meaning: To turn away one's eyes; to prevent something bad from happening.
words.example: Quick thinking averted a major disaster.

words.meaning: A feeling of great respect mixed with fear or wonder.
words.example: They stood in awe of the great waterfall.

words.meaning: To cause someone to be completely puzzled.
words.example: The question baffled even the experts.

words.meaning: Lacking strong features or characteristics; uninteresting.
words.example: The food was bland and tasteless.

words.meaning: Perfect happiness; great joy.
words.example: It was sheer bliss to relax on the beach.

words.meaning: Noisy, energetic, and cheerful.
words.example: The children were boisterous at the playground.

words.meaning: Showing a willingness to take risks; confident.
words.example: She made a bold decision to start her own company.

words.meaning: To improve or increase something.
words.example: A good night's sleep can boost your energy.

words.meaning: A load, especially a heavy one; a duty or responsibility.
words.example: Financial debt can be a heavy burden.

words.meaning: An event causing great and often sudden damage or distress.
words.example: The earthquake was a calamity for the region.

words.meaning: Not showing or feeling nervousness or excitement.
words.example: He remained calm during the emergency.

words.meaning: The quality of being open and honest; frankness.
words.example: She appreciated his candor about the situation.

words.meaning: Careful to avoid potential problems or dangers.
words.example: Be cautious when crossing busy roads.

words.meaning: Complete disorder and confusion.
words.example: The storm caused chaos across the city.

words.meaning: To protect and care for lovingly.
words.example: She cherished every moment with her family.

words.meaning: Relating to a city or town, especially its administration.
words.example: Voting is a civic duty.

words.meaning: Relating to ordinary citizens and their concerns.
words.example: Civil rights are protected by the constitution.

words.meaning: To state or assert that something is the case.
words.example: He claimed to have seen the accident.

words.meaning: Logical and consistent; clearly expressed.
words.example: She gave a coherent explanation of the problem.

words.meaning: To recall and show respect for someone or something.
words.example: A statue was built to commemorate the hero.

words.meaning: The state of being dedicated to a cause or activity.
words.example: Success requires commitment and hard work.

words.meaning: To share or exchange information, news, or ideas.
words.example: It's important to communicate clearly with your team.

words.meaning: A group of people living in the same place.
words.example: The local community came together to help flood victims.

words.meaning: Having the necessary ability or knowledge to do something effectively.
words.example: She is a highly competent professional.

words.meaning: To grasp mentally; to understand something fully.
words.example: It was difficult to comprehend the scale of the disaster.

words.meaning: To admit that something is true or valid after initial resistance.
words.example: He conceded that he was wrong.

words.meaning: To have discussions; to grant something to someone.
words.example: The committee will confer a prestigious award.

words.meaning: Feeling or showing certainty about something.
words.example: She was confident in her ability to win.

words.meaning: To face up to and deal with a problem.
words.example: She had to confront her fears head on.

words.meaning: Permission for something to happen or agreement to do something.
words.example: You need parental consent to participate.

words.meaning: To communicate or make known a message or information.
words.example: It's hard to convey emotion through text alone.

words.meaning: Warm and friendly.
words.example: They had a cordial relationship despite their differences.

words.meaning: Advice, especially that given formally.
words.example: She sought counsel from a trusted advisor.

words.meaning: To try to acquire or develop a quality or skill.
words.example: Cultivate good habits from an early age.

words.meaning: Having or showing skill in achieving one's ends by deceit.
words.example: The fox is known as a cunning animal.

words.meaning: Adventurous or audaciously bold.
words.example: It was a daring rescue mission.

words.meaning: Settling an issue; producing a definite result.
words.example: The general made a decisive move in the battle.

words.meaning: To become smaller, fewer, or less; to decrease.
words.example: Air quality has declined in many cities.

words.meaning: To arrive at a fact or conclusion by reasoning.
words.example: From the clues, the detective deduced who had done it.

words.meaning: To put off an action or event to a later time.
words.example: The meeting was deferred until next week.

words.meaning: Not having enough of a specified quality or ingredient.
words.example: Her diet was deficient in vitamins.

words.meaning: To engage in long and careful consideration.
words.example: The jury deliberated for three days before reaching a verdict.

words.meaning: Great pleasure and happiness.
words.example: The children's laughter was a delight to hear.

words.meaning: The action of declaring something to be untrue.
words.example: The company issued a denial of the allegations.

words.meaning: To publicly declare something to be wrong or evil.
words.example: The president denounced the terrorist attack.

words.meaning: To reduce significantly in quantity, quality, or strength.
words.example: Overuse of fertilizers can deplete the soil.

words.meaning: To move or fall downward.
words.example: The hikers began to descend the mountain at dusk.

words.meaning: Worth having or wanting; pleasing, attractive.
words.example: Good communication skills are desirable in any job.

words.meaning: Very loving or loyal; dedicated.
words.example: She was devoted to her students and their well-being.

words.meaning: The state or quality of being worthy of honor and respect.
words.example: Every person deserves to be treated with dignity.

words.meaning: Careful and persistent work or effort.
words.example: She achieved success through diligence and perseverance.

words.meaning: To perceive or recognize something clearly.
words.example: It's hard to discern the truth from the rumors.

words.meaning: The practice of training people to obey rules.
words.example: Self-discipline is key to achieving long-term goals.

words.meaning: The unjust treatment of different categories of people.
words.example: Racial discrimination is illegal in most countries.

words.meaning: To decide that something is not worth considering.
words.example: Don't dismiss an idea without first hearing it out.

words.meaning: To interrupt normal course of something.
words.example: The storm disrupted the power supply.

words.meaning: Recognizably different in nature from something else.
words.example: The two flavors have distinct characteristics.

words.meaning: To separate from another path and go in a different direction.
words.example: Their opinions began to diverge on that issue.

words.meaning: To be the most important or main factor in something.
words.example: Technology companies dominate the global economy.

words.meaning: Striking in appearance or effect; exciting.
words.example: There was a dramatic improvement in her health.

words.meaning: Able to withstand wear, pressure, or damage; hard-wearing.
words.example: These boots are durable and waterproof.

words.meaning: Resulting from or showing sincere and intense conviction.
words.example: He was earnest in his desire to help others.

words.meaning: To develop or present a theory in further detail.
words.example: Could you elaborate on your idea?

words.meaning: Authority or power given to someone to do something.
words.example: Education is a tool for the empowerment of women.

words.meaning: To surround and have or hold within.
words.example: The course encompasses a wide range of topics.

words.meaning: To compel observance of or compliance with a law or rule.
words.example: Police officers enforce the law.

words.meaning: To participate or become involved in something.
words.example: She likes to engage in meaningful conversations.

words.meaning: To increase or further improve the quality of something.
words.example: Adding music enhanced the overall experience.

words.meaning: To give greater knowledge or understanding to someone.
words.example: The lecture enlightened the audience on climate change.

words.meaning: To improve or enhance the quality or value of something.
words.example: Travel enriches your perspective on life.

words.meaning: To make certain that something will occur.
words.example: Please ensure that all doors are locked before leaving.

words.meaning: The state of being equal in status, rights, and opportunities.
words.example: All people should be treated with equality.

words.meaning: Relating to moral principles or the branch of knowledge dealing with them.
words.example: Doctors are bound by strict ethical guidelines.

words.meaning: To be exceptionally good at something.
words.example: She excels in both academics and sports.

words.meaning: To carry out or put into effect a plan or order.
words.example: The team executed the strategy flawlessly.

words.meaning: To use up completely; to tire out completely.
words.example: Running the marathon exhausted him completely.

words.meaning: To publicly display a work of art or item of interest.
words.example: Her paintings were exhibited in a local gallery.

words.meaning: To make an action happen sooner or be accomplished more quickly.
words.example: We need to expedite the delivery process.

words.meaning: Very unusual or remarkable; beyond what is ordinary.
words.example: She has an extraordinary ability to learn quickly.

words.meaning: Filled with or characterized by a lively energy and excitement.
words.example: The crowd was exuberant after the team's victory.

words.meaning: Treating people equally without favoritism.
words.example: A fair trial is a basic right for everyone.

words.meaning: Well known from long or close association.
words.example: The song sounded familiar to her.

words.meaning: To attract and hold the attention of someone.
words.example: History fascinates her deeply.

words.meaning: Possible to do easily or conveniently.
words.example: The plan was feasible given the available resources.

words.meaning: Having or displaying a passionate intensity.
words.example: She was a fervent supporter of the cause.

words.meaning: Ready and able to change so as to adapt to different circumstances.
words.example: A flexible schedule helps maintain work-life balance.

words.meaning: The centre of interest or activity.
words.example: Education was the main focus of the conference.

words.meaning: To refuse to allow something.
words.example: The school forbids the use of mobile phones in class.

words.meaning: The ability to predict what will happen and prepare for it.
words.example: He had the foresight to plan for the future.

words.meaning: Direct and outspoken; straightforward and honest.
words.example: She was forthright about her expectations.

words.meaning: Sparing or economical with money or food.
words.example: Living a frugal lifestyle helps save money.

words.meaning: To achieve or realize something desired or promised.
words.example: She fulfilled her dream of becoming a doctor.

words.meaning: Simple elegance or refinement of movement.
words.example: She danced with grace and poise.

words.meaning: Feeling or showing appreciation for kindness received.
words.example: She was grateful for all the help she received.

words.meaning: To feel deep sadness, especially at someone's death.
words.example: She grieved the loss of her beloved dog.

words.meaning: The process of increasing in size, number, or importance.
words.example: The company showed strong growth in the last quarter.

words.meaning: To keep a thought or feeling secretly in one's mind.
words.example: Don't harbor negative feelings.

words.meaning: Severe suffering or privation.
words.example: The family endured great hardship during the drought.

words.meaning: To control and make use of natural resources or abilities.
words.example: We must harness the power of solar energy.

words.meaning: A system in which members are ranked according to relative status.
words.example: Every organization has its own hierarchy.

words.meaning: Characterized by the treatment of the whole person rather than parts.
words.example: She prefers a holistic approach to health and wellness.

words.meaning: Free of deceit and untruthfulness; sincere.
words.example: Always be honest with yourself and others.

words.meaning: High respect; great esteem.
words.example: It is an honor to receive this award.

words.meaning: A feeling of expectation and desire for something to happen.
words.example: She held on to hope during difficult times.

words.meaning: Friendly and welcoming to strangers or guests.
words.example: The locals were hospitable and kind.

words.meaning: To put forward as a hypothesis.
words.example: Scientists hypothesize about the origins of life.

words.meaning: To regard or represent as perfect or better than in reality.
words.example: We tend to idealize people we admire.

words.meaning: Treating all rivals or disputants equally; fair.
words.example: A judge must remain impartial during a trial.

words.meaning: To indicate or suggest something without stating it explicitly.
words.example: His silence seemed to imply agreement.

words.meaning: To encourage or stir up violent or unlawful behavior.
words.example: The speaker attempted to incite the crowd.

words.meaning: Certain to happen; unavoidable.
words.example: Change is inevitable in life.

words.meaning: To make changes in something established; to introduce new ideas.
words.example: Companies must innovate to stay competitive.

words.meaning: The capacity to gain an accurate and deep understanding.
words.example: The book gave great insight into human behavior.

words.meaning: To fill with the urge to do or feel something creative.
words.example: Her speech inspired thousands of young people.

words.meaning: To combine one thing with another so they become a whole.
words.example: The school aims to integrate technology into learning.

words.meaning: Intention or purpose.
words.example: His intent was to help, not to harm.

words.meaning: To explain the meaning of something.
words.example: She interpreted the poem differently from her classmates.

words.meaning: To have as a necessary part or consequence.
words.example: The job involves a lot of travel.

words.meaning: To cause someone to be alone or apart from others.
words.example: The illness forced her to isolate herself for two weeks.

words.meaning: An act of travelling from one place to another.
words.example: Life itself is a journey full of discoveries.

words.meaning: Feeling, expressing, or causing great pleasure and happiness.
words.example: Her joyful laughter filled the room.

words.meaning: Having, showing, or done with good judgment or sense.
words.example: Make judicious use of your time and energy.

words.meaning: Having a strong interest or enthusiasm; enthusiastic.
words.example: She is keen on learning new languages.

words.meaning: To arouse or inspire an emotion or feeling.
words.example: Reading can kindle a love of learning.

words.meaning: A significant and historically important event or discovery.
words.example: The treaty was a landmark in diplomatic history.

words.meaning: Conforming to the law or to rules; lawful.
words.example: She had legitimate reasons for her actions.

words.meaning: To set someone free from a situation that limits freedom.
words.example: Education can liberate people from poverty.

words.meaning: Of imposing height; of a noble or elevated character.
words.example: She had lofty ambitions for her future.

words.meaning: Expressed clearly; easy to understand.
words.example: His explanation was lucid and easy to follow.

words.meaning: An official order or commission to do something.
words.example: The government has a mandate to reduce carbon emissions.

words.meaning: To display or show a quality or feeling by one's acts.
words.example: Her talent manifested early in life.

words.meaning: Having reached an advanced stage of mental or emotional development.
words.example: She is mature beyond her years.

words.meaning: To intervene between parties in a dispute to bring about agreement.
words.example: A neutral party was brought in to mediate the conflict.

words.meaning: Showing great attention to detail; very careful and precise.
words.example: She is meticulous about her work.

words.meaning: Conscious or aware of something; attentive.
words.example: Be mindful of others' feelings.

words.meaning: Unassuming in the estimation of one's abilities or achievements.
words.example: Despite his success, he remained modest.

words.meaning: The impetus and driving force gained by the development of a process.
words.example: The project gained momentum after the first success.

words.meaning: Concerned with the principles of right and wrong behavior.
words.example: She made a moral decision to speak the truth.

words.meaning: Experienced or done by each of two or more parties toward the other.
words.example: Their friendship was built on mutual respect.

words.meaning: Required to be done or achieved; essential.
words.example: Hard work is necessary for success.

words.meaning: To try to reach an agreement or compromise by discussion.
words.example: Both sides agreed to negotiate a peace deal.

words.meaning: Not supporting or helping either side in a conflict; impartial.
words.example: Switzerland has traditionally remained neutral in conflicts.

words.meaning: To provide with the food or other substances necessary for growth.
words.example: A healthy diet nourishes the body.

words.meaning: A goal or aim; a thing aimed at or sought.
words.example: Our main objective is to improve customer satisfaction.

words.meaning: Most conducive to a favourable outcome; best.
words.example: Exercise is best done at the optimum time of day.

words.meaning: A painful or horrific experience.
words.example: Survivors described the earthquake as a terrifying ordeal.

words.meaning: The determination of the relative position of something.
words.example: The new employees attended an orientation session.

words.meaning: The extent or length of reaching out; community service.
words.example: The charity has an active outreach program.

words.meaning: More important than anything else; supreme.
words.example: Safety is of paramount importance on a construction site.

words.meaning: To continue in a course of action despite difficulty.
words.example: Persevere through challenges and you will succeed.

words.meaning: To cause someone to do something through reasoning or argument.
words.example: He persuaded her to try a new approach.

words.meaning: The desire to promote the welfare of others through charity.
words.example: Many millionaires engage in philanthropy.

words.meaning: Seeming reasonable or probable.
words.example: Her explanation was plausible and well thought out.

words.meaning: To commit by a solemn promise; to vow.
words.example: She pledged to support the community.

words.meaning: Creating or controlling a situation rather than just responding to it.
words.example: A proactive manager prevents problems before they occur.

words.meaning: Very great or intense; having great insight or depth.
words.example: Reading has had a profound impact on her outlook.

words.meaning: Done without delay; quick and immediate.
words.example: A prompt response to the email was appreciated.

words.meaning: To put forward a plan for consideration.
words.example: She proposed a new solution to the problem.

words.meaning: Flourishing financially; successful.
words.example: They lived a prosperous and comfortable life.

words.meaning: A set of reasons or logical basis for a course of action.
words.example: She explained the rationale behind her decision.

words.meaning: A cause, explanation, or justification for an action.
words.example: There is always a reason behind every decision.

words.meaning: To restore friendly relations; to make compatible.
words.example: The two friends reconciled after their argument.

words.meaning: To make changes in order to improve something.
words.example: The government plans to reform the education system.

words.meaning: To strengthen or support, especially with additional material.
words.example: Praise reinforces positive behavior in children.

words.meaning: Closely connected or appropriate to the matter at hand.
words.example: Make sure your answer is relevant to the question.

words.meaning: Unceasingly intense; continuing without stopping.
words.example: Her relentless effort finally paid off.

words.meaning: Consistently good in quality or performance; dependable.
words.example: A reliable friend is always there when you need them.

words.meaning: To resume something after an interruption; to begin again.
words.example: She renewed her commitment to exercise.

words.meaning: Having a good reputation; respected and trusted.
words.example: Choose a reputable company for your investments.

words.meaning: Able to withstand or recover quickly from difficult conditions.
words.example: Children are often more resilient than we think.

words.meaning: To settle or find a solution to a problem.
words.example: They worked together to resolve the conflict.

words.meaning: Having the ability to find quick and clever ways to overcome difficulties.
words.example: A resourceful person can solve problems creatively.

words.meaning: To continue to have something; to keep possession of.
words.example: She retained her composure throughout the meeting.

words.meaning: To feel deep respect or admiration for something.
words.example: He was revered by his students and colleagues.

words.meaning: Strong and healthy; vigorous.
words.example: A robust economy benefits everyone in the country.

words.meaning: Regarded with great respect and reverence by a particular religion.
words.example: The temple is a sacred place of worship.

words.meaning: To examine or inspect closely and thoroughly.
words.example: The auditors scrutinized every financial record.

words.meaning: The state of being calm, peaceful, and untroubled.
words.example: She found serenity in nature after a stressful day.

words.meaning: Not easily convinced; having doubts or reservations.
words.example: She was skeptical about the new policy at first.

words.meaning: Willing to talk and engage in activities with other people.
words.example: He is a sociable person who enjoys meeting new people.

words.meaning: Formal and dignified; serious.
words.example: They made a solemn promise to each other.

words.meaning: Possessing supreme or ultimate power.
words.example: Every nation has sovereign control over its territory.

words.meaning: Performed or occurring as a result of a sudden impulse.
words.example: They made a spontaneous decision to travel abroad.

words.meaning: Resolutely or dutifully firm and unwavering.
words.example: She remained steadfast in her beliefs.

words.meaning: To raise levels of physiological or nervous activity; to encourage.
words.example: Good books stimulate creative thinking.

words.meaning: Of such excellence, grandeur, or beauty as to inspire great admiration.
words.example: The sunset was truly sublime.

words.meaning: So delicate or precise as to be difficult to analyze or describe.
words.example: There was a subtle difference between the two versions.

words.meaning: Enough; adequate for a specific need.
words.example: Five hours of sleep is not sufficient for most adults.

words.meaning: To give a brief statement of the main points of something.
words.example: She summarized the key findings of the report.

words.meaning: Higher in rank, status, or quality.
words.example: This product is of superior quality.

words.meaning: To forcibly put an end to something; to prevent from being expressed.
words.example: She tried to suppress her laughter during the meeting.

words.meaning: Perceptible by touch; clear and definite; real.
words.example: There are tangible benefits to regular exercise.

words.meaning: Tending to keep a firm hold; persistent.
words.example: He was tenacious in pursuing his goals.

words.meaning: The ability to accept things that one disagrees with or dislikes.
words.example: Tolerance and respect are essential in a diverse society.

words.meaning: Easy to perceive or detect; open and honest.
words.example: A transparent government earns the trust of its citizens.

words.meaning: Being or happening at the end of a process; final.
words.example: The ultimate goal is to create a sustainable future.

words.meaning: All in full agreement.
words.example: The committee reached a unanimous decision.

words.meaning: To weaken or lessen the effectiveness or power of something.
words.example: Gossip can undermine trust in a workplace.

words.meaning: To confirm or support something which has been questioned.
words.example: The court upheld the original decision.

words.meaning: To make practical and effective use of something.
words.example: We should utilize all available resources wisely.

words.meaning: Great courage in the face of danger, especially in battle.
words.example: The soldier was awarded a medal for his valor.

words.meaning: To make sure or demonstrate that something is true, accurate, or justified.
words.example: Please verify your email address.

words.meaning: Strong, healthy, and full of energy.
words.example: She goes for a vigorous run every morning.

words.meaning: Having or showing high moral standards.
words.example: He led a virtuous life dedicated to serving others.

words.meaning: Absolutely necessary or important; essential.
words.example: Oxygen is vital for human survival.

words.meaning: Exposed to the possibility of being attacked or harmed.
words.example: Children are among the most vulnerable members of society.

words.meaning: Feeling or showing caution about possible dangers or problems.
words.example: Be wary of strangers asking for personal information.

words.meaning: The quality or state of being prepared to do something.
words.example: Her willingness to help made a big difference.

words.meaning: Having or showing great energy or enthusiasm in pursuit of a cause.
words.example: She is a zealous advocate for children's rights.
